rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MIKE O'REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant To Provide Security Pursuant To Ccp 391.1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANTS DAN NOLTING and PINE GROVE GROUP, INC.'S Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant To Provide Security Pursuant To CCP 391.1. Granted. Plaintiff is deemed a vexatious litigant per Code of Civil Procedure section 391.1 and it is not probable that he will prevail against moving Defendants. Plaintiff must post a bond in the amount of $30,000 no later than September 26, 2012. If the bond is not timely posted, moving Defendants may seek dismissal per an ex parte application. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving parties without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY G. INGRAM VS. 24HR FITNESS CENTERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT TOM WELTER'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to all three claims alleged against Mr. Welter. The defamation claim is time barred per Code of Civil Procedure 340(c). The fraud claim does not allege an actionable misrepresentation or justifiable reliance and lacks the requisite particularity. The discrimination claim does not allege facts showing a discriminatory intent and plaintiff's conclusory conspiracy allegations are insufficient to support the claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

WEST, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF OTHERS, KARIN CAHILL, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF OTHERS' Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Denied. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CRAIG JOYNER VS. YAT-PANG AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ROBERT JOYNER MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: COUPONS.COM INCORPORATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 6, RESPONDENTs HELLMAN & FRIEDMAN, LLC and HELLMAN & FRIEDMAN CAPITAL PARTNERS VI, L.P.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Denied. Respondent is not entitled to fees under either Civil Code section 1717 or Maryland Rule 1-341. § 8.1 of the Settlement Agreement provides for an award of fees issued only by an arbitrator and not by the court. The evidence does not support a finding that Petitioner engaged in intentional misconduct as required for an award of fees per Maryland Rule 1-341. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: COUPONS.COM INCORPORATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax Resp.'S Costs; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 6, PETITIONER COUPONS.COM INCORPORATED'S Motion To Tax Respondents' Costs. Granted as to Line 10 costs are granted in part as to Line 13 costs. Line 13 costs are taxed in the amount of $ 4,660.98. Attorney fees are stricken in accordance with the denial of Respondents' motion for attorney fees. Online legal research, word processing and routine expenses for local travel are not recoverable under Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5, while the photocopying expenses are recoverable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE C. WEBB VS. LORRAINE O. LEGG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANTS LORRAINE O. LEGG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GENERAL PARTNER OF 3234 WASHINGTON STREET ASSOCIATES, and 3234 WASHINGTON STREET ASSOCIATES' Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To First Amended Complaint. Granted on the following conditions: 1) plaintiff has until 7 days before trial to conduct discovery on the new affirmative defenses and cross-complaint and may propound written discovery as to those matters requiring responses within ten days; 2) Plaintiff may take a second deposition of Ms. Legg on the new affirmative defenses and cross-complaint; and 3) plaintiff may file a motion for summary judgment as to the new affirmative defenses and cross-complaint per the schedule set forth in Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b) and may notice the motion to be heard on or before October 29, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF SI CHAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT. Denied. If the motion were granted, defendant would likely file a motion to strike the request for punitive damages which would be granted. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BRITT DOYLE VS. UBS FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANTS UBS FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., WENDI ECKHARDT, DON TRABERT and JOE ASIANO'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. Granted. Plaintiff's claims against UBS arise out of his business activities with UBS and thus those claims are within the scope of the Arbitration Agreement contained in the Form U4 and FINRA Code of Arbitration Procedure Rule 13200. Plaintiff's claims against the individual Defendants are also arbitrable because the individual Defendants are alleged to be employees of Defendant UBS and to have acted within the course and scope of their employment when making the allegedly defamatory statements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 11, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT ROBERT GRAHAM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. Moving party did not timely file the demurrer per Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). Moving party did not file proof of service per California Rule of Court 3.1300(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
